payload.answer: Rejected. Do not commit an entire transcript as one memory card. Durable memory should be atomic, salient, and structured; an 8000-word transcript in a single conversation_summary card is overbroad, likely contains transient or irrelevant details, and may preserve ambiguity or superseded statements as if current.

entity_resolution.reason: A whole transcript stored as a single conversation_summary card is not an appropriate durable memory unit and provides no extracted, atomic, user-relevant facts to resolve to entities.

repair_options: Extract only durable, user-relevant facts from the transcript into separate atomic memory cards.
Use appropriate memory kinds rather than a single conversation_summary when facts concern preferences, profile details, goals, relationships, constraints, or projects.
Preserve uncertainty by marking low confidence or ambiguous statements instead of flattening the transcript into one asserted summary.
Exclude ephemeral dialogue, assistant reasoning, repeated text, irrelevant details, and any superseded or deleted facts.
If a conversation summary is needed, make it concise and scoped to durable outcomes only, not a transcript dump.
